title: ColdRelay vs Mailreef — Cold Email Infrastructure Comparison
description: Comparing ColdRelay and Mailreef for cold email infrastructure. See pricing, dedicated server approach, deliverability features, and scalability side-by-side.

overview.coldrelay: ColdRelay provisions Microsoft 365 mailboxes on dedicated, isolated Azure tenants with dedicated IPs. Pricing is per-mailbox: $1 (1-199), $0.85 (200-999), $0.70 (1K-4,999), $0.55 (5K+). Each setup includes 100-150 mailboxes per domain, automated SPF/DKIM/DMARC, no warmup wait, and a 99% inbox placement guarantee. Minimum 50 mailboxes with 2-4 hour setup.
overview.competitor: Mailreef markets itself as the most stable and reliable cold email infrastructure, built by cold emailers for cold emailers. They provide dedicated mail servers with dedicated IPs, unlimited free inboxes, and BYO domain support. Their single plan — Agency Flex — costs $249/month per server plus $0.001/send. Mailreef emphasizes 99.9% uptime, zero blocked mailboxes, and claims 100M+ emails sent monthly across their platform. Features include 1-click domain purchases, 1-click mailbox creation, auto DNS, delivery consulting, and integration with Smartlead and Instantly.

featureComparison.competitor: $249/month per server + $0.001/send — flat server fee plus usage

featureComparison.competitor: 150+ mailboxes per server, unlimited free inboxes per server

featureComparison.competitor: Dedicated mail server with dedicated IP address — full server ownership
Auto-create SPF, DKIM, DMARC with 1-click setup

1-click domain and mailbox creation — rapid provisioning

sections.coldrelayWins: ColdRelay's per-mailbox pricing with no per-send fees makes costs completely predictable. At 150 mailboxes sending 50K emails/month, ColdRelay costs $150 vs. Mailreef's $299 — nearly half the price. The lower entry point ($50 for 50 mailboxes vs. $249/month minimum) makes ColdRelay accessible to teams that want to start small and scale. ColdRelay also avoids the variable cost uncertainty of per-send pricing — you know your exact monthly cost regardless of sending volume. The 99% inbox guarantee with a money-back promise provides concrete deliverability assurance.
sections.competitorWins: Mailreef's dedicated server approach gives you true server-level ownership — not just isolated tenants, but actual dedicated mail servers with your own IP. The live delivery consulting is a genuine differentiator: having real experts available to help optimize your sending is invaluable, especially when troubleshooting deliverability issues. Mailreef's spammer screening protects the ecosystem by actively filtering bad actors, which helps maintain IP reputation for legitimate users. The unlimited mailboxes per server model is attractive for teams that want to maximize density without per-inbox costs. Their 99.9% uptime claim and 100M+ monthly email volume suggest battle-tested infrastructure.
sections.whoShouldChooseColdRelay: Choose ColdRelay if you want predictable per-mailbox pricing without per-send surprises, a low minimum commitment, and the confidence of a 99% inbox guarantee. ColdRelay is ideal for teams scaling from small to large operations who want straightforward cost planning — especially high-volume senders who would rack up per-send fees on Mailreef.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Mailreef if you want a dedicated mail server with live delivery consulting and don't mind the $249/month minimum and per-send fees. Mailreef is well-suited for established agencies already sending at scale (100K+ emails/month) who value expert deliverability support and full server control. If you're sending over 250K emails/month on a single server, the unlimited mailbox model can be cost-effective.
sections.verdict: Both platforms deliver quality cold email infrastructure with dedicated IPs and automated DNS. ColdRelay wins on pricing transparency, lower entry barrier, and absence of per-send fees — making it the better choice for cost-conscious teams and those scaling up. Mailreef wins on the managed service side with live delivery consulting and a dedicated server model that appeals to high-volume operations willing to pay the premium.

faqs.question: How does Mailreef's pricing compare to ColdRelay at scale?
faqs.answer: At 150 mailboxes sending 50K emails/month: ColdRelay costs $150 vs. Mailreef's $299. At 300 mailboxes (2 servers on Mailreef) sending 200K/month: ColdRelay costs $255 ($0.85/mailbox) vs. Mailreef's $698 ($498 servers + $200 sends). ColdRelay is significantly cheaper at most volumes.

faqs.question: Does Mailreef charge per email sent?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mailreef charges $0.001 per email sent on top of the $249/month server fee. This means sending 100,000 emails costs an additional $100/month. ColdRelay has no per-send charges.

faqs.question: Which offers better deliverability support?
faqs.answer: Mailreef includes live delivery consulting with real experts. ColdRelay focuses on delivering high-quality infrastructure with its 99% inbox guarantee. If you want hands-on expert support, Mailreef has the edge. If you want guaranteed results, ColdRelay's inbox guarantee is more concrete.

faqs.question: Can I start with a small setup on Mailreef?
faqs.answer: Mailreef's minimum is $249/month for one server. ColdRelay lets you start with as few as 50 mailboxes ($50/month), making it much more accessible for smaller operations or testing.

faqs.question: Does Mailreef use Microsoft 365 like ColdRelay?
faqs.answer: Mailreef uses their own dedicated mail servers rather than Microsoft 365. ColdRelay uses Microsoft 365 on isolated Azure tenants. Both approaches have merits — Microsoft 365 benefits from Microsoft's reputation, while dedicated servers give full control.
